candidate.title: [Sell What?]
candidate.description: Groups siblings that distinguish the selling activity by the kind of thing being sold: information, physical objects, or services.
candidate.activityDistinction: These members answer 'What is being sold?' about Sell; they are not kinds of one single narrower activity because information, physical objects, and services are co-values along the object-of-sale axis.

h2.rationale: The proposed members are not kinds of an activity called [Sell What?] but co-values answering what is sold under Sell, so the correct structure is a facet-collection with mechanism facet.
h2.weakness: Sell (Other) also appears to answer the what-sold axis, so excluding it from the facet weakens the partition.

candidate.concept: All of these denote the generic concept of a sellable physical good with no further category distinction.

judge.name: CanonicalTitleChooser
judge.rationale: Sell Supply can denote supplies or provisions used as inputs, a meaningfully narrower category with different purchasing context than the catch-all Sell Items, Sell Merchandise, and Sell Product, so the set should not be merged.
